wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the profitmaker project. Explain its purpose as a customizable cryptocurrency trading terminal supporting over 100 exchanges via CCXT integration. Describe the high-level architecture including the widget-based UI, Zustand state management, and real-time data acquisition system. Document key features such as multi-exchange account management, order execution, portfolio tracking, and theme customization. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase like 'widgets', 'data providers', 'dashboard', and 'trading terminal'. Provide practical examples of common user workflows from setup to trading. Illustrate the system context with diagrams showing component relationships and data flow between UI, stores, and external services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Base UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the base UI components library used throughout the profitmaker application. Explain how these components are built on top of Radix UI primitives with added Tailwind CSS styling and project-specific customizations. Document each component's purpose, props interface, and usage patterns with practical examples from the codebase. Describe the design system principles including spacing, typography, color palette, and responsive behavior. Illustrate how form components (input, select, checkbox) integrate with React Hook Form through the form.tsx abstraction. Detail accessibility features implemented across components like keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, and screen reader support. Provide guidance on extending existing components or creating new ones that follow established conventions for class naming, prop types, and state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Market Data Acquisition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for this sub-feature focused on how market data is acquired in profitmaker. Explain the dual-path architecture supporting both WebSocket (real-time) and REST (polling/fallback) data fetching strategies. Document the subscription lifecycle from widget request to data normalization and caching. Detail the role of dataProviderStore in managing active subscriptions and deduplication across exchanges. Include code examples showing how widgets initiate data requests and handle streaming updates. Address error handling for network interruptions, rate limiting, and malformed responses. Discuss performance implications of each strategy and guidance on choosing appropriate methods based on use case.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Store
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the dashboardStore.ts in profitmaker. Explain its role in managing the state of dashboards and widgets, including layout persistence, widget addition/removal, and position/resizing operations. Document the use of Zustand with Immer middleware for immutable state updates and structured selectors to optimize re-renders. Detail the integration with WidgetContext for cross-component state sharing and drag-and-drop functionality via useWidgetDrag hook. Provide examples of how components like WidgetSimple interact with the store. Address common issues such as layout desynchronization and performance optimization when handling multiple widgets. Include guidance on extending the store for custom widget types or advanced layout configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the theme customization system in profitmaker. Explain how the theme context is implemented using React Context API to manage dark/light mode state across the application. Document the use of CSS variables for dynamic theming and how they are defined in index.css. Describe the useTheme hook that enables components to access and toggle themes programmatically. Provide examples of integrating theme switching in UI components like the SettingsDrawer and UserDrawer. Include guidance on adding new theme variants, extending the color system, and ensuring theme persistence using localStorage. Address common issues such as flash of incorrect theme during load and strategies to prevent it.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the dashboard management system in the profitmaker trading terminal. Explain how users can create, customize, and persist widget-based dashboards using a drag-and-drop interface. Document the underlying Zustand store architecture that manages layout state, widget configurations, and persistence via localStorage. Describe the WidgetContext provider that enables cross-component communication for widget operations. Include implementation details of dashboard serialization, rehydration, and responsive layout handling. Provide practical examples of adding, resizing, moving, and removing widgets. Illustrate integration points with other systems such as theme management and user preferences. Address common issues like layout corruption, performance bottlenecks with many widgets, and cross-browser compatibility. Offer optimization tips for rendering large numbers of concurrent widgets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Creating Custom Widgets
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for creating custom widgets in the profitmaker application. Thoroughly explain how to use the WidgetContext API to access dashboard state and widget lifecycle events. Document the required interface contracts defined in dashboard.ts that new widgets must implement. Include concrete examples from WidgetSimple.tsx demonstrating component structure, props handling, and integration patterns. Explain how to register new widgets in the index.ts export system. Address common issues such as state synchronization, resize behavior, and settings persistence. Provide guidance on implementing responsive design and accessibility compliance within the widget architecture. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chart Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Chart widget, which provides interactive financial charting capabilities using the NightVision library. Explain how it integrates with useDataProviderStore to fetch and display real-time candlestick data from multiple exchanges via CCXT. Document the dual data loading strategy: REST API for initial historical data and WebSocket for live updates. Detail the infinite scroll implementation that dynamically loads historical data as users pan left on the chart. Describe the responsive resize behavior using ResizeObserver and debounced NightVision instance recreation. Cover configuration management through chartWidgetStore for persistent timeframe settings. Include examples of overlay generation, volume toggle functionality, and error handling during initialization. Address performance considerations around data deduplication and memory management when appending new candles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the technology stack used in profitmaker. Detail the core frameworks including React for UI rendering, TypeScript for type safety, Vite for build performance, and Zustand for state management. Explain the role of CCXT library in enabling connectivity to 100+ cryptocurrency exchanges. Document the UI component architecture built on Radix UI primitives with Tailwind CSS for styling. Cover the data visualization stack using Recharts for charting components. Include information about testing utilities, linting configuration, and development tooling. Provide version compatibility details and dependency relationship diagrams. Explain the rationale behind technology choices and their impact on developer experience and application performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trading Widgets System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for the trading widgets system that forms the core interactive elements of the profitmaker terminal. Detail how each widget encapsulates specific trading functionality such as charting, order book visualization, trade execution, and portfolio monitoring. Explain the common Widget wrapper pattern that provides consistent drag-and-drop capability, resizing handles, header controls, and settings integration. Document the data flow from Zustand stores to individual widgets and how configuration is managed through settings wrappers (e.g., ChartSettingsWrapper, UserBalancesSettingsWrapper). Provide concrete examples showing how MarketDataWidget consumes real-time data from dataProviderStore and how OrderForm integrates with orderExecutionService. Include best practices for implementing new widgets following existing patterns for performance optimization, error handling, and responsiveness.

wiki_catalogs.name: CCXT Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for CCXT integration in profitmaker. Describe the abstraction layer that enables seamless connectivity to over 100 cryptocurrency exchanges via the CCXT library. Document the differences between ccxtBrowserProvider and ccxtServerProvider, including security trade-offs and CORS considerations. Explain how exchange instances are managed, cached, and authenticated using API keys. Provide implementation details on request routing, response normalization, and error mapping across heterogeneous exchange APIs. Include practical examples of integrating a new exchange and handling exchange-specific quirks. Address security best practices for credential storage and transmission.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Provider Store
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for dataProviderStore.ts, which manages market data acquisition and distribution across the application. Describe its architecture using Zustand stores combined with action files (fetchingActions, dataActions) and provider implementations (ccxtBrowserProvider, ccxtServerProvider). Explain the data flow from user-configured subscriptions through WebSocket/REST interfaces to CCXT-based exchange connectivity. Document strategies for caching, deduplication, and subscription lifecycle management. Illustrate how utils like webSocketUtils handle real-time message parsing and error recovery. Provide code examples showing integration with widgets like Chart or OrderBookWidget. Address challenges such as connection throttling, rate limiting, and failover between provider types. Include performance considerations for high-frequency data updates and memory usage optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the styling implementation in profitmaker, focusing on the integration of Tailwind CSS with a utility-first approach. Explain the configuration in tailwind.config.ts including custom color palettes optimized for trading terminal readability, typography settings, and spacing scales. Document how global styles are managed in index.css and how they interact with Tailwind's reset and layer system. Cover the organization of UI components in the ui directory and their consumption of Tailwind classes. Provide examples from components like button.tsx, card.tsx, and table.tsx to illustrate consistent styling patterns. Include performance considerations such as PurgeCSS configuration, class name optimization, and minimizing bundle size. Address accessibility aspects including focus states, motion preferences, and high contrast modes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trading Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for this sub-feature focused on the trading functionality of the profitmaker platform. Thoroughly explain the order placement workflow including market, limit, stop-loss, and take-profit orders through the OrderForm component. Document the interaction between placeOrderStore, orderExecutionService, and CCXT providers for secure trade submission. Detail how real-time order book data from OrderBookWidget integrates with trade execution decisions. Explain position tracking, active orders display, and trade history visualization in TradesWidget. Include concrete code examples showing state transitions during order submission, confirmation, and error handling. Address configuration options for order routing across multiple exchanges, slippage tolerance, and order lifetime settings. Discuss common failure modes (e.g., insufficient balance, network timeouts) and their resolution strategies. Provide performance considerations for low-latency trading scenarios and UI responsiveness during high-frequency updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Extending Data Providers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for extending data providers in profitmaker. Thoroughly explain the implementation details of the ccxtServerProvider and how to create new provider types that integrate with the existing data pipeline. Document the interfaces and utility functions in providerUtils.ts that facilitate provider creation. Include concrete examples from CCXT_SERVER_WIDGET_INTEGRATION.md showing server-side integration patterns. Explain how new providers connect to the dataProviderStore for state management and distribution. Address common issues like error handling, connection lifecycle management, and data normalization across different exchange APIs. Provide guidance on testing provider integrations and ensuring compatibility with the widget ecosystem. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: OrderBook Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the OrderBookWidget, which displays real-time order book depth with bid/ask visualization and spread calculation. Explain its integration with useDataProviderStore to subscribe to order book data streams from selected exchanges. Document the depth configuration options (5-50 levels) and cumulative volume calculations for market depth analysis. Describe the visual representation of price levels with gradient coloring for bid and ask sides. Cover the responsive layout that adapts to different screen sizes and the performance optimization techniques used for high-frequency update rendering. Include implementation details of the OrderBookSettingsWrapper for configuring exchange, symbol, and depth parameters. Provide usage examples showing how users can monitor liquidity and identify support/resistance levels.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on setting up and running the profitmaker application. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including prerequisites like Node.js and package installation via npm. Document the process of configuring environment variables, particularly API keys for exchange connectivity. Walk through launching the development server using Vite and accessing the application interface. Include basic usage examples such as navigating to the Trading Terminal, creating a dashboard, and adding initial widgets. Explain the entry point architecture starting from main.tsx through App.tsx to page components. Address common setup issues and their solutions, including CORS configuration and API key validation errors. Provide troubleshooting tips for development environment problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket vs REST Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content comparing WebSocket and REST data strategies in profitmaker. Explain when each protocol is used based on data type (e.g., order book updates vs historical OHLCV). Document the connection management system, including reconnection logic, heartbeat monitoring, and fallback mechanisms. Illustrate message formats for real-time updates and batch responses. Include performance benchmarks, memory usage considerations, and battery impact on client devices. Provide guidance on configuring refresh intervals and subscription densities. Demonstrate how the application handles disconnections and state recovery with minimal data loss.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Store
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for userStore.ts, responsible for managing user accounts, API credentials, and authenticated exchange sessions. Detail how it securely stores exchange keys using environment-aware encryption patterns and integrates with ccxtAccountManager and ccxtInstanceManager to instantiate per-exchange clients. Explain the login/connection workflow, session persistence, and multi-account switching capabilities. Document interactions with SettingsDrawer and UserDrawer components for UI-driven configuration. Provide examples of safely retrieving authenticated providers for trading operations. Address security best practices for credential storage, token expiration handling, and protection against XSS attacks. Include troubleshooting steps for common authentication failures and connection timeouts.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Account Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on user account management in the profitmaker application. Explain how users securely store and manage API keys for over 100 cryptocurrency exchanges using both browser-based and server-side CCXT providers. Document the role of userStore in managing multi-account authentication states, exchange connectivity, and credential isolation. Describe the security model differences between ccxtBrowserProvider (client-side API calls) and ccxtServerProvider (proxy through backend). Illustrate the user workflow for adding, testing, and removing exchange accounts via UserDrawer interface. Include implementation details of encrypted storage, session management, and permission scoping. Address common issues like invalid API keys, IP whitelisting conflicts, rate limiting, and two-factor authentication interference. Provide best practices for securing credentials, rotating keys, and monitoring unauthorized access attempts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for performance optimization in profitmaker. Thoroughly explain implementation details for optimizing high-frequency market data updates using WebSocket connections and efficient state updates. Document best practices demonstrated in dashboardStore.ts for minimizing re-renders through proper state slicing and selector patterns. Include concrete examples from useDataProvider.ts showing data fetching strategies and caching mechanisms. Explain how requestLogger.ts can be used to identify performance bottlenecks in network communications. Address common issues like memory leaks in event listeners, excessive store subscriptions, and UI jank during intensive data processing. Provide guidance on optimizing WebSocket message handling and batch processing of market data updates. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trades Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the TradesWidget, which displays a real-time feed of executed trades with filtering and sorting capabilities. Explain its subscription mechanism to trade data streams through useDataProviderStore and CCXT integration. Document the filtering options by trade side (buy/sell), price range, and volume thresholds. Describe the sorting functionality by time, price, and volume with auto-scrolling to latest trades. Cover the UI design choices for trade row highlighting based on aggressor side and the performance optimizations for handling high-volume trade streams. Include implementation details of the TradesSettingsWrapper for configuring symbol, exchange, and display limits. Provide examples of how traders use this widget to detect market momentum and large institutional orders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the component architecture of profitmaker. Explain the separation between base UI components in the ui directory and domain-specific trading widgets in the widgets directory. Describe the composition pattern where atomic UI elements (buttons, inputs, cards) are combined into complex trading interfaces. Document the Widget wrapper component that provides common functionality like drag handles, resize controls, and header actions. Illustrate how WidgetContext enables communication between parent containers and child widgets. Provide examples of component reuse patterns across different widget types. Include best practices for creating new components following existing conventions for props, styling, and responsiveness.

wiki_catalogs.name: Widget-Specific Stores
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for all widget-specific Zustand stores in the application. Cover chartWidgetStore (managing chart configurations and timeframes), orderBookWidgetStore (handling order book depth and display settings), tradesWidgetStore (storing executed trade history), userBalancesWidgetStore (tracking portfolio values), userTradingDataWidgetStore (aggregating positions and orders), placeOrderStore (managing order form state), groupStore (color and grouping logic), notificationStore (toast and alert queueing), and settingsDrawerStore (UI state for configuration panels). For each, explain its purpose, key state properties, interaction with parent dashboardStore, and relationship to corresponding React components. Provide usage examples showing how hooks consume these stores. Address concerns like state bloat, unnecessary re-renders, and synchronization with global user or data provider state.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for this sub-feature focused on the notification system within the profitmaker trading terminal. Explain the architecture of real-time alerts using Sonner and Toast components for transient user feedback. Document how notificationStore manages both ephemeral notifications and persistent historical logs accessible through NotificationHistory. Cover event types including trade executions, order status changes, price alerts, and system warnings. Include implementation details of toast customization (duration, positioning, theming), stacking behavior, and dismissal controls. Describe the data model for stored notifications including timestamps, categories, and associated metadata. Provide usage examples for developers integrating new notification sources into the system. Address accessibility requirements for screen readers, keyboard navigation, and reduced motion preferences. Discuss performance considerations when handling high-volume notification streams and strategies for batch processing or throttling.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Balances Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the UserBalancesWidget, which visualizes cryptocurrency portfolio distribution through pie charts and balance summaries. Explain its integration with userStore to access multi-exchange account credentials and fetchBalance operations via CCXT. Document the aggregation logic that combines balances across different exchanges and converts values to a common quote currency. Describe the interactive pie chart implementation with hover details and click-to-filter functionality. Cover the settings configuration in UserBalancesSettingsWrapper for selecting accounts, grouping options, and refresh intervals. Include security considerations around API key handling and rate limiting strategies to prevent exchange bans. Provide examples of portfolio rebalancing analysis and net worth tracking across volatile markets.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the state management system in profitmaker. Describe the use of Zustand for global state management across dashboards, user accounts, market data, and trading functions. Explain the modular store architecture with separate files for dashboard, data provider, user, and widget-specific states. Document the use of Immer middleware for immutable updates and structured selectors for performance optimization. Illustrate the flow of data from user interactions through store actions to state updates. Provide examples of store usage patterns with hooks like useDashboardStore and useUserStore. Address common state management challenges such as synchronization between related stores and handling asynchronous operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Market Data Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MarketDataWidget, which provides comprehensive market overview information including tickers, price changes, and trading volumes across multiple instruments. Explain its data acquisition pattern using useDataProviderStore to subscribe to ticker streams from various exchanges. Document the grid-based layout that efficiently displays numerous trading pairs with color-coded price movements. Describe the filtering and search functionality that allows users to quickly locate specific markets. Cover the performance optimization techniques for handling hundreds of concurrent WebSocket connections and updating the UI efficiently. Include implementation details of the refresh mechanisms and error recovery strategies when data feeds disconnect. Provide examples of how traders use this widget to scan for breakout opportunities and monitor correlated assets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow & Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on data flow and third-party integrations in profitmaker. Detail the mechanisms for acquiring market data through both WebSocket and REST APIs using the CCXT library. Explain the data provider architecture that supports multiple connection strategies (browser vs server). Document the request lifecycle from subscription initiation to data normalization and distribution to widgets. Cover the order execution flow from UI input through validation to exchange submission. Illustrate error handling patterns for network failures and rate limiting. Include security considerations for transmitting sensitive trading data and API credentials.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deals Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the DealsWidget, which tracks and displays completed trading transactions with detailed execution analytics. Explain its integration with dataProviderStore to retrieve historical deal records from connected exchanges via CCXT's fetchMyTrades API. Document the tabular presentation of deals with sortable columns for date, instrument, side, price, amount, and fees. Describe the DealDetails panel that provides in-depth information about individual transactions including order hierarchy and slippage analysis. Cover the filtering capabilities by date range, instrument, and trade direction. Include implementation details of pagination and lazy loading for handling large trade histories. Provide examples of performance reporting, tax preparation, and strategy backtesting using the data presented in this widget.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the core features of the profitmaker trading terminal. Explain the dashboard management system that allows users to create, customize, and persist widget layouts. Document the trading functionality including order placement, position monitoring, and trade history viewing. Describe the multi-account management capabilities for connecting and switching between exchange accounts. Cover the notification system with both real-time alerts and historical logging. Illustrate how these features work together to provide a complete trading workflow from market analysis to execution and portfolio review.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Trading Data Widget
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the UserTradingDataWidget, which consolidates personal trading activity across multiple exchanges into a unified interface. Explain its authentication flow using userStore to securely access API keys and establish connections to exchange accounts. Document the data aggregation process that normalizes trade formats from different exchanges into a consistent schema. Describe the time-series visualization of trading volume and P&L metrics over customizable periods. Cover the settings configuration in UserTradingDataSettingsWrapper for selecting accounts, date ranges, and calculation methodologies. Include privacy considerations around local data storage and encryption of sensitive information. Provide examples of behavioral analysis, fee optimization, and performance benchmarking against market indices using this comprehensive trading history view.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theming & Styling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the theming and styling system in profitmaker. Explain the implementation of dark/light theme toggling using React context and CSS variables. Document the Tailwind CSS configuration and utility-first approach to styling. Describe the color palette system designed for terminal applications with high contrast and readability. Cover accessibility features including proper contrast ratios, keyboard navigation support, and screen reader compatibility. Provide guidance on customizing themes and extending the design system. Include performance considerations for style loading and runtime theme switching.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Topics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on advanced development topics in profitmaker. Explain the process of creating custom widgets by leveraging the WidgetContext API and adhering to the component contract. Document extension points for adding new data providers beyond the existing CCXT implementations. Cover performance optimization techniques for handling high-frequency market data updates. Address security considerations when dealing with user API keys and trading operations. Provide guidance on debugging complex state interactions and network communications. Include patterns for testing complex user interactions and asynchronous data flows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on contributing to the profitmaker codebase. Document the development environment setup including required tools, dependencies, and configuration steps. Explain the build process managed by Vite and the TypeScript compilation pipeline. Cover coding standards enforced by ESLint and formatting rules. Provide testing strategies including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios. Document the contribution workflow from branch creation to pull request submission. Include debugging techniques and recommended developer tools. Address deployment considerations for both development and production environments.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>
# Profitmaker: Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

Profitmaker is an open-source, customizable cryptocurrency trading terminal designed to support over 100 crypto exchanges. The platform enables users to manage their trading activities with customizable dashboards and widgets while ensuring security by keeping API keys on the user's local machine.

**Core Goals and Objectives:**
- Provide a modular, extendable trading interface
- Support real-time market data from 100+ exchanges via CCXT integration
- Enable customization through dashboards and widgets
- Facilitate development of trading bots
- Maintain user security by keeping sensitive credentials locally

**Target Audience:**
- Cryptocurrency traders seeking a customizable terminal
- Developers building trading algorithms and bots
- Users who prioritize security and control over their trading infrastructure

Sources:
- [README.md](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/README.md)

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

The application follows a modern React-based architecture with Zustand for state management, implementing a modular component system centered around trading widgets and data providers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[App] --> B[ThemeProvider]
    A --> C[QueryClientProvider]
    A --> D[BrowserRouter]
    D --> E[Index Route]
    D --> F[Test Routes]
    E --> G[TradingTerminal]
    G --> H[TabNavigation]
    G --> I[Widget Management]
    G --> J[Dashboard Store]
    I --> K[Multiple Widget Types]
```

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

- **State Management**: Uses Zustand with Immer and persistence middleware for global state
- **Dependency Injection**: Provider pattern for data services (CCXT Browser/Server)
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Subscription model for market data updates
- **Component Composition**: Widget-based UI architecture with dynamic rendering

### Data Flow Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Interaction] --> B[Widget Components]
    B --> C[Zustand Stores]
    C --> D[Data Provider System]
    D --> E[CCXT Integration]
    E --> F[Exchange APIs]
    F --> E
    E --> G[WebSocket/REST]
    G --> C
    C --> H[UI Components]
    H --> I[Visualizations]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap and root rendering
- `src/App.tsx`: Root component with providers and routing
- `src/pages/TradingTerminal.tsx`: Primary trading interface

### Core Modules
- `src/store/dashboardStore.ts`: Dashboard and widget layout management
- `src/store/dataProviderStore.ts`: Market data acquisition and distribution
- `src/store/userStore.ts`: User account and exchange credential management
- `src/components/widgets/`: Reusable trading components

### Configuration Approach
- Vite-based build configuration (`vite.config.ts`)
- Tailwind CSS for styling (`tailwind.config.ts`)
- ESLint for code quality (`eslint.config.js`)
- TypeScript configuration with multiple tsconfig files

### External Dependencies
- `package.json`: Defines dependencies including React, Zustand, CCXT, and UI libraries

### Integration Points
- CCXT library for exchange connectivity
- WebSocket and REST APIs for real-time data
- Local storage persistence for user preferences
- Environment-specific server integration

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Frontend] --> B[Vite Build System]
    A --> C[Tailwind CSS]
    A --> D[Zustand State]
    D --> E[CCXT Integration]
    E --> F[100+ Exchanges]
    D --> G[Local Storage]
    A --> H[Express Server]
```

Sources:
- [main.tsx](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/main.tsx)
- [App.tsx](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/App.tsx)
- [TradingTerminal.tsx](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/pages/TradingTerminal.tsx)
- [dashboardStore.ts](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/store/dashboardStore.ts)
- [dataProviderStore.ts](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/store/dataProviderStore.ts)
- [userStore.ts](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/store/userStore.ts)
- [package.json](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/package.json)

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- Customizable dashboards with drag-and-drop widgets
- Real-time market data visualization
- Multi-exchange account management
- Trading order execution capabilities
- Portfolio and balance tracking
- Historical data analysis with infinite scrolling

### Implementation Highlights
- Dynamic widget system with type-based rendering
- Intelligent data fetching method selection (WebSocket vs REST)
- Responsive design with mobile support
- Theme customization and accessibility features
- Comprehensive error handling and logging

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Dashboard
    Dashboard --> WidgetSelection: Add Widget
    WidgetSelection --> WidgetConfiguration: Configure
    WidgetConfiguration --> DataSubscription: Subscribe
    DataSubscription --> DataProcessing: Process
    DataProcessing --> Visualization: Display
    Visualization --> UserInteraction: Interact
    UserInteraction --> DataSubscription: Update Request
    UserInteraction --> Dashboard: Save Layout
    Dashboard --> Persistence: Store State
```

Sources:
- [TradingTerminal.tsx](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/pages/TradingTerminal.tsx)
- [dashboardStore.ts](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/store/dashboardStore.ts)
- [dataActions.ts](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/store/actions/dataActions.ts)
- [subscriptionActions.ts](/Users/suenot/projects/profitmaker/src/store/actions/subscriptionActions.ts)
</blog>
